title: Code Points, Grapheme Clusters, Character Categories, and Labels · Traceback Repair Bay
summary: A preserved Bookbot run contains one plausible path, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, reporting, CLI, testing, performance, packaging, security, or release defect; identify the first failed boundary before changing code. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in honestly labeled text-metrics panel and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and…
objectives: Distinguish bytes, code points, extended grapheme clusters, display cells, letters, and stakeholder-defined characters.
Use Unicode categories or explicit predicates to separate letters, marks, numbers, punctuation, symbols, separators, and controls.
State whether character frequencies operate on original or normalized text, preserve case, and include or exclude marks.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Order the regression proof · Debug Metric Label Evidence
steps.instruction:
Order the Metric Label Evidence Bookbot and Python corpus-to-release evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Order the regression proof · Debug Metric Label Evidence.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Metric Label Evidence assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Bookbot and Python Order the regression proof · Debug Metric Label Evidence, keep Metric Label Evidence connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.text: A debug Bookbot team handles case 2577 during a clean-environment package release. Build deterministic Python evidence to label and reconcile code-point, grapheme, and letter metrics without overstating browser segmentation; retain source permission, digest purpose, revision, Python, Unicode, policy, command, and output identity; then install the built wheel in a fresh environment and rerun success, invalid-argument, and decode-failure cases. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Label every metric with unit, inclusion policy, input version, and reconciliation or non-reconciliation rule.

content.text: Order work so Metric Label Evidence has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Metric Label Evidence
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: bookbot-metric-label-evidence-270jp3" that uses case 8545 to label and reconcile code-point, grapheme, and letter metrics without overstating browser segmentation. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open host files, run processes or Git, install packages, read credentials or host state, publish artifacts, or cause external effects; verify native boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, Unicode 17, disposable files and environments, real CLI and package runs, profiling, accessibility, security, load, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Metric Label Evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Metric Label Evidence into observable performance evidence. For Bookbot and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Metric Label Evidence, keep Metric Label Evidence connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-metric-label-evidence-270jp3
# Competency: bookbot-metric-label-evidence.
# Case 8545: an assistive-technology terminal review.
# Operating constraint: retain source permission, digest purpose, revision, Python, Unicode, policy, command, and output identity.
# Changed case: install the built wheel in a fresh environment and rerun success, invalid-argument, and decode-failure cases.
def evidence_bookbot_metric_label_evidence_270jp3(code_points: int = 14, graphemes: int = 11, letters: int = 9, labels_explicit: bool = True, segmentation_transfer_named: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_270jp3 = "270jp3-3pj072-8545"
    assert evidence_variant_270jp3
    evidence_axes_270jp3 = (
        "corpus270jp3",
        "policy3pj072",
        "revision8545",
        "command270jp38545",
        "report3pj0728545",
        "test8545270jp3",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_270jp3)) == 6
    if min(code_points, graphemes, letters) < 0:
        return False, code_points, "metric-count"
    if graphemes > code_points or letters > code_points:
        return False, graphemes, "unit-reconciliation"
    if not labels_explicit:
        return False, letters, "ambiguous-unit"
    if not segmentation_transfer_named:
        return False, graphemes, "grapheme-overclaim"
    assert letters <= code_points
    return True, graphemes, "honest-text-metrics"
